Josh’s comments on HC change…interesting
stories.summary:
An article I just read quotes Josh as follows:



	 



	“We’ve obviously got a new head coach, and so to have those memories being made in a new spot,” Allen said. “I think change is needed sometimes, especially when we’ve been close in the past, even going back to 20-ish, 30-ish years ago, just how close the Bills have been to winning a Super Bowl — I think this is kind of a switch that was necessary and I’m very excited.”



	 



	Wow. This is the first time I’m aware of that Josh has been this definitive (publicly) on the topic.



	 



	I’m really expecting some exciting and good things this year.

stories.title: Terrel Bernard's "Horrible Contract Extension"
stories.summary:
I'm sure many of you listen to Locked On Bills. This week, Joe was lamenting how the Bills are stuck with Terrel Bernard's contract and it's one of Beane's worst.



	 



	Someone please correct me if I'm wrong, but this is how I read his contract:



	 



	2026: $5.35M Cap Hit



	2027: $11.15M Cap Hit



	2028: $12M Cap Hit, but he can be released and save $5.5M 



	 



	Yes, I understand he's often injured. However, he flashed more playmaking potential than Edmunds ($18M/yr with the Bears) ever did.



	Obviously respected in the locker room to be 1 of 2 team captains and he just turned 27 years old.



	$5M this year, $11M next year, and a $6.5M dead cap hit the following year if we want to cut him isn't that bad.



	 



	I'm sick of hearing this is a terrible contract we can't get away from.

stories.title: Extremely interesting Athletic piece: when players peak, by position
stories.summary:
https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7426208/2026/07/06/at-what-age-do-nfl-players-in-different-positions-peak/
	 



	Lots of surprises here. If history is any guide, we’ve got a lot of prime Josh still in store, and DJ Moore will be in his peak years for a couple more seasons. Lots to chew on and debate here as it applies to the Bills too.



	——



	 



	’On defense, the inside of the line is just a different beast. Paul Dehner Jr. explored how DTs are aging better than ever, speaking with the Bengals’ 28-year-old addition Dexter Lawrence, who’s now alongside the 31-year-old Jonathan Allen. They explained why their cohort has been aging so well:



	💬 “You don’t lose strength,” Allen said. “That’s the last thing to go. That’s something I’m still able to rely on. And if anything, I feel stronger than ever.”



	“It’s (also) an understanding of how offenses work, an anticipation of what they are going to do can help you so much more than a cornerback, where if you don’t have the speed to keep up with a go ball, it’s going to be hard at 35 to keep up with receivers just coming into the league.”’

stories.title: Throwback Thursday Bills at Browns ‘89 Playoffs
stories.summary:
Yes, it’s a loss. But because Cleveland was scoring at will early, OC Ted Marchabroda switched tactics and began employing a no huddled, rapid paced offense. Cleveland didnt know how to respond and Kelly and the Offense began driving at an unprecedented high level, enabling the Bills to fight back into the game. Unfortunately, Ronnie Harmon dropped a game winning TD catch just before the last play. But the game had a lasting change as the next year the Bills employed a hurry up offense with no huddles and took the League by storm. With the late addition of the venerable James Lofton, the Offense began exhibiting a never before seen explosion that catapulted our undeniable ascension to a terrifying level that led directly to 4 straight Super Bowls! Yes, our young stars shone brightly. Kelly, Thomas, Reed, Lofton, BRUUUUCE, Bennett, Conlan, Leonard Smith and an OL that absolutely dominated opposing Defense. 
	The K-gun made it’s initial debut here and would last nearly a decade.



	 



	ENJOY!

stories.title: James Cook is borderline first-round fantasy pick, according to ESPN
A glimpse of huge potential of area surrounding new Buffalo Bills stadium
Brandin Cooks remains in contact with Bills and wants to come back
stories.summary:
Extensive story in The Athletic: https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/7425977/2026/07/09/nfl-bills-sean-mcdermott-brandin-cooks-catch/



	 



	‘He wants to return to the Bills, and the interest apparently is mutual. Bills football boss Brandon Beane said in March the team had stayed in contact with Cooks’ camp, and Cooks said he talked with new head coach Joe Brady recently.



	 



	Any contract will likely be team friendly. Cooks doesn’t need the money. He has earned $124.9 million over his career, less than just nine receivers all time. He flies his own Cirrus Vision Jet, although he’s in the market for a new one. The Oregon home where he lives with his college-sweetheart wife, Briannon, and their three children was featured in Architectural Digest two years ago.



	 



	But you can’t buy a Super Bowl ring.



	 



	“I know what teams don’t know right now,” Cooks’ agent, Ryan Tollner, said. “He’s working in the dark right now as hard as anyone you could imagine.



	 



	“He’s a master of controlling what he can control. Even the way the season ended, he was crushed because he wants to win, but I would guess that Brandin would rather have the opportunity to make a play and have the ball come to him. That’s what he works so relentlessly for.”



	Tollner said plenty of contenders have extended offers, that an exciting choice will be made, but Cooks has emphasized his wish for one team in particular. Cooks is done rewinding that replay, but he wants to run it back with Buffalo.



	 



	Training camp, Cooks said, is “the purest form of football,” so he intends to be on a roster within the next few weeks.



	 



	“Obviously, Buffalo is the place I’d love to be,” Cooks said. “I want to prove that to them and have a full offseason with them. Both sides are figuring things out. We’ll see, but hopefully something transpires because I love going to training camp. That’s where you build that callus.”‘

Batavia Party Zone
stories.summary:
What say you all about us advocating for a corner of the new stadium to be officially (or unofficially, if we must) recognized as the “Batavia Party Zone”?



	We not too old people remember a time, before the league was whitewashed, when fans could actually tape signs on The Stadium Wall (no pun intended because it’s true) and one of those signs every home game was…”The Batavia Party Zone…” It was unmistakable and existed proudly during every game in OP for about 15-20 years. I’m feeling nostalgic tonight, is anyone else smelling what I’m cooking?

stories.title: Who are some of your favorite players from over the years???
stories.summary:
I'm a millennial, so my memory only goes back so far, but I'll start with Lee Evans.. I think that if he played in this offense, in his prime, he would be a perennial 1,000yd + receiver.. He played with with terrible QBs throughout his career.. One of the most explosive WRs I think in franchise history, he was a threat to break one every time.. With Josh he would be making the Pro Bowl every year..



	 



	 



	Nate Clements in my mind was the best CB in Bills history.. Many people throw the term "shutdown corner" around but in all actuality it implies to very few players.. Nate Clement was the epitome of a shutdown corner, for the first 5-6 years of his career he was absolutely amazing.. He fell off after he went to San Francisco if I remember, but still, he was pretty much the best corner in the NFL for a solid 5 years straight....



	 



	 



	Other random players: Jason Peters, Travis Henry, Stevie Johnson, Ryan Fitzpatrick, LeSean McCoy, Ted Washington, Pat Williams, Takeo Spikes, London Fletcher, Devin Singletary and so many more..



	 



	 



	 



	Who do you guys got????

stories.title: If you weren't thrilled about the Brady hire as HC do you feel any better about it now?
stories.summary:
Well it was 5 months ago yesterday he was announced as HC.



	 



	 



	When he got hired I'll admit my reaction was very negative.  I think off the emotion of the Broncos loss, the messy press conference over the firing of McDermott, and my feeling was at the time the Bills didn't really interview a lot of candidates.  



	 



	 



	While I'm still not sold about the hire my feeling months removed from it all is that I'm willing to roll with him as HC.

stories.title: The View from Patriots Wire
stories.summary:
Came across this article:
	
	“The Patriots have seemingly pulled away in the race with the many moves they've made in the offseason.



	 



	Meanwhile, the Bills haven't done much of anything to signal they'll be any better than they were last year. One could even argue that they'll take a step back after firing head coach Sean McDermott. Josh Allen is Superman on the football field, but he's going to need more than D.J. Moore and a middle-of-the-road defense to fly high enough to reach his first Super Bowl….



	 



	The Bills have to be seeing the moves being made by the Patriots, and it would be hard to imagine them feeling good about their offseason in relation to the competition. They are now faced with a situation where the Patriots have one of the best young quarterbacks in the league in Drake Maye and a top-10 defensive unit that gave Allen problems last season.



	 



	Will the Bills ever wake up and realize fighting fire with fire is much better than laying the hopes and dreams of an entire franchise on the shoulders of one man?”



	 



	Do you think this is justified or comically arrogant?
